items.title: Boag's beer brand not for sale, but owner open to offers

children.content: The union representing Boag's brewery workers in Launceston wants its owner, Lion Australia, to sell the brand along with the brewery. But the company's response has left workers confused.

gallery.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.
workers in orange jumpers outside an old red brick building holding signs petitioning for their brewery to not close down

thumbnail.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.
workers in orange jumpers outside an old red brick building holding signs petitioning for their brewery to not close down

items.title: Infants now eligible for free meningococcal B vaccine in Tasmania

children.content: Vaccinations for the rare but potentially deadly meningococcal B disease are now free for all Tasmanian infants for the next four years.

image.altText: An adult hand holds a needle to the arm of a small child, who has a restful, neutral expression.

items.title: Renewed push to ban 'conversion practices' in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

image.altText: A large rainbow flag is waved in the air.

items.title: Neil the seal’s antics make him popular, but there’s a big risk

children.content: Neil the seal has become one of Tasmania's biggest celebrities. But authorities are concerned his popularity could put him and the public at risk.

image.altText: A large elephant seal leans the front of his body on a timbe beach fence at night.

images.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

children.content: Facing World Cup elimination at the hands of DR Congo, England needed a hero to step up when it mattered most. Harry Kane was that man.

image.altText: Harry Kane from behind holds out his hands in front of a crowd

children.content: Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2, while Harry Kane strikes twice after half-time to defeat DR Congo 2-1. Follow live.

items.title: Influencers to be targeted in crackdown on gambling advertising

children.content: The Albanese government will target social media influencers and individuals who promote gambling on social media as part of its proposed laws restricting advertising.

image.altText: A woman with the parliament house flag in the background.

items.title: NSW sergeant 'snuck a look' for ex-cop mate accused of domestic violence

children.content: A NSW Police officer has admitted to unlawfully accessing the force's internal database to locate details about a domestic violence complaint against his friend.

image.altText: Photo illustration with police officer silhouette and text messages from story

images.altText: Anthony Albanese and Jodie Haydon walk to Midwinter Ball.
Clegg, left, wears a black sheer shirt over a slip. Taylor, right, wears a black tuxedo

children.content: The federal government has backed down on its aged care assessment tool to determine how much funding older Australians get. Follow live.

items.title: Thousands of survivors of child sexual abuse face looming redress backlog

children.content: At its current processing rate, the scheme will not be able to clear its backlog before it closes.

image.altText: Chris Coombes

items.title: ASX opens lower as oil price drops

children.content: The ASX opens lower following a sea of red on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Picture of stock market board with ASX sign at the top left corner.

items.title: Before and after images show widespread impact of Venezuelan quakes

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

gallery.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

items.title: Alleged people smuggler to face court over remote boat arrivals

children.content: The federal government says a group of foreign nationals has been removed from Australia after reaching Far North Queensland illegally by boat this week.

image.altText: CCTV footage shows two men ordering from Weipa bakery.

items.title: Djokovic apologises for pranking ball kid during 'vintage' Wimbledon win

children.content: A relaxed Novak Djokovic takes time out between sets of his second-round Wimbledon win over Stefanos Tsitsipas to play a prank on a ball kid.

image.altText: Novak Djokovic pranks a ball kid at Wimbledon.

items.title: Jackass began with a gun shot; now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum came from but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

image.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Russian daredevil couple scale Empire State Building in proposal stunt

children.content: Two Russian daredevils, Angela Nikolau and Vanya Beerkus, claim to be the duo who scaled the 102-storey, 443-metre tower for an apparent show of love and a marriage proposal.

gallery.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

thumbnail.altText: Two people in black clothing and masks standing atop a skyscraper antenna with a black protest banner in the wind.

items.title: US Navy crew member missing as chopper goes down in Arabian Sea

children.content: A US Navy service member is missing and three others are injured after their MH-60S Sea Hawk helicopter went down in the Arabian Sea.

image.altText: A US Navy grey fighter helicopter hovering above the deck of an aircraft carrier, where three people in jackets are standing.

items.title: Two people arrested after death of teenager outside Melbourne hospital

children.content: The boy was found by emergency services workers with serious injuries outside a medical centre in Craigieburn, but died at the scene.

image.altText: Craigieburn Community Hospital

items.title: Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover says he has Alzheimer's

children.content: Danny Glover, who turns 80 in July,  has revealed that he has Alzheimer's disease and is leaning on his family for support as he lives with the incurable condition.

image.altText: Danny Glover is in a black suit and patterned bowtie, standing by a red wall with the word Oscars written on it.

items.title: 'My last Wimbledon': Kyrgios bows out with typically erratic doubles outing

children.content: Australian Nick Kyrgios says he has almost certainly played his final Wimbledon match after losing in the first round of the men's doubles alongside Alexander Bublik.

image.altText: Nick Kyrgios runs forward during a doubles match at Wimbledon. Alexander Bublik is behind him.

items.title: Spider venom spray could save Australia's honey bees from varroa mites

children.content: Queensland researchers are testing novel ways to kill varroa mites, which have developed a resistance to standard treatments.

gallery.altText: Two middle-aged men in a laboratory. One holds up a tank with a spider in it, the other a specimen jar.
A very large varroa mite on a bee.
A beekeeper walking with a smoker through bee hives.

items.title: Extremists using 'codes' to evade detection online, royal commission hears

children.content: Far-right individuals adopt memes as a way to push "more palatable" antisemitism, which is less likely to be picked up by social media moderators.

image.altText: Hannah Rose in a blazer, with brunette hair, speaking before the commission.

items.title: XPeng electric vehicle owners still chasing $5k cashback months on

children.content: When Mustansir Milky took the leap and bought a new electric vehicle, he didn't expect to have to fight to get the $5,000 cashback payment he was promised.

image.altText: Mustansir Milky stands beside his electric car outside his house

items.title: 'At greatest risk of CTE': Expert calls out dangers of heading the ball

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

gallery.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

thumbnail.altText: A soccer player in blue and orange heads a ball in front of trees in a park.

items.title: No mice found after drone baiting on remote WA island

children.content: A world-first drone-baiting program to protect migratory bird populations shows early signs of success off Western Australia's north coast.

image.altText: A man holding a mousetrap

items.title: Dingo control stopped to protect cultural significance

children.content: Farmers voice concern for their flocks after wild dog control is halted on jointly managed conservation land in parts of the Wheatbelt.

image.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

items.title: As main street shops vanish, could they help solve housing shortages?

children.content: As businesses shut up shop in regional main streets, the government is weighing up whether they could help solve the housing crisis.

items.title: Traditional owner takes government to court over flood plain project

children.content: A protest camp on the banks of the Murray River enters its sixth week as traditional owners and locals fight a plan to return water to nine flood plains they say will kill trees and wildlife.

image.altText: Two women holding protest signs against a floodplain project

items.title: What to know about rail industrial action as reduced timetables continue

children.content: After months of negotiations and disruptions to south-east train services, Queensland Rail has reached an in-principle agreement with three unions.

image.altText: A train at a platform at Roma Street Station in Brisbane

items.title: Fireworks light up the NT's skies on annual cracker night

children.content: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters busy putting out blazes.
